title: SCC Shadow Protocol — Training Classroom

missions.title: Deploy Training Framework (shadow_protocol.py, practice_gym.py, umep_registry.json)
deliverables: epos/training/shadow_protocol.py — ShadowProtocol class (compare_files, score_merit, score_mission, generate_training_pairs, track_merit)
epos/training/practice_gym.py — PracticeGym class (seed_session, run_scc_exam, run_mission_shadow, commit_grade)
context_vault/training/umep_registry.json — 30 model slots (12 named), certification levels 0-5
All 4 files deployed to epos-core container and verified importable

missions.title: Seed sandbox with 20260413-03A exam
deliverables: 11 SCC stub files staged (.scc.py — shebang only)
11 baseline files staged (.baseline.py — Desktop CODE implementations)

missions.title: Run SCC exam — 3-try protocol
missions.finding: SCC PRIMARY produced shebang-only stubs for all 11 required files across all 3 exam tries. Merit ceiling decay (Try1=100%, Try2=85%, Try3=70%) had no effect — stub penalty returns merit=5 regardless.

missions.title: Pattern analysis — Desktop CODE audit
missions.root_cause: SCC delegation via docker exec produced shebang-only output. SCC does not yet translate directive spec context into full Python module implementations.

missions.title: Generate training pairs → 20260413-03A_patterns.jsonl
deliverables: Pattern: STUB — no implementation produced, frequency 11/11 (100%)
QLoRA target: full-file generation from directive spec

what_went_well: All 3 training framework modules imported cleanly on first try — no dependency issues
UMEP registry updated live: SCC-PRIMARY promoted 0→1 (Attempt) automatically by commit_grade()
Merit timeline append worked — track_merit() wrote to merit_timeline.jsonl without error
30-slot registry with 12 named specialists gives clear model taxonomy for multi-model training
3-try exam protocol confirmed: stub penalty is merit=5 regardless of try — correct behavior
Shadow run + training pair generation completed in single invocation of run_mission_shadow()
what_went_wrong: docker cp fails when target directory doesn't exist in container — required mkdir first, then cp with trailing slash syntax (cp dir/. container:/path/)
compare_files() key 'similarity_ratio' doesn't exist — actual key is 'matching_lines_pct'. Print script had wrong key.
generate_training_pairs() uses 'bad_example'/'good_example' keys not 'bad'/'good' — minor key mismatch in debug print only, not functional
Pattern structure from score_mission() is {'pattern': str, 'frequency': int, 'files_pct': int} — not {'type': str, ...}. Print loop used wrong key in first run.
Stub pattern string 'STUB — no implementation produced' is not in PATTERN_LESSONS — gets default empty bad/good examples. Training pair lesson text is correct but examples are thin.
what_was_learned: docker cp directory requires: first mkdir in container, then cp with source as 'dir/.' — NOT 'dir/' as destination key
SCC PRIMARY baseline merit: 5/100 on all files — full stub. Training curriculum must start at zero-to-implementation generation.
Stub detection in score_merit() correctly assigns merit=5 regardless of try multiplier — the penalty is applied before the multiplier would matter
UMEP certification auto-promotion works correctly: commit_grade() evaluates cert level and promotes 0→1 on first attempt
Pattern-level training (1 pair per recurring gap, not per file) is the right abstraction — 11 stubs produce 1 training target, not 11 duplicates

training_summary.qlora_curriculum_priority: Full Python module generation from directive specification context
training_summary.next_exam: Re-examine 20260413-03A after QLoRA fine-tuning on training pairs

pending.action: Use training pairs to fine-tune SCC model — stub→implementation generation
Add 'STUB — no implementation produced' to PATTERN_LESSONS with concrete bad/good examples showing shebang stub vs full module
